Motivation
Followup of #5619.

Modifications
Current main branch will cause bug when enabling MTP for Deepseek-V3.  This PR fixes this by adding support of loading fused_qkv_a_proj for DeepSeek draft models.


Test

python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model /dev/shm/DeepSeek-V3 --tp 8 --specula

vllm.title: [Chore] Remove Sampler from Model Code
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR removes the sampler and the sample method from the model, and moves it to the model runner.
Currently, the sample method is just repeated for all models, with little to no difference between models.
This PR reduces this redundancy and makes it easier to change the sampler interface in the fu

vllm.title: Simplify `TokenizerGroup`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As discussed on Slack this PR:

removes RayTokenizerGroupPool, BaseTokenizerGroup, TokenizerPoolConfig
Simplifies the fetching of tokenizer groups now that they will always be TokenizerGroup

vllm.title: Fix OOT registration test
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#16988 changed the error that's raised when an OOT model fails to load.
This PR updates the expected error message.

vllm.title: [V1][PP] Optimization: continue scheduling prefill chunks
vllm.author: ruisearch42
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is based on #13637 (the first commit) and fixes the race condition in test_engine_core (the second commit).
The DummyEngine in test_engine_core has a race condition because the threads from different execute_model() calls can run out of order, and vLLM engine core expects the batches to fini

vllm.title: [Misc] Remove OLMo2 config copy
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Olmo2 config has been included in current transformers release, so we don't need to hold this copy.

vllm.title: Improve static type checking in `LoRAModelRunnerMixin`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Static type checkers do do not know if nn.Module contains a config attribute.
The information was in model_config anyway.

vllm.title: [V1][Structured Output] Clear xgrammar compiler object when engine core shut down to avoid nanobind leaked warning
vllm.author: shen-shanshan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Clear xgrammar compiler object when engine core shut down to avoid nanobind leaked warning.
Find more details about the bug here: #16951.
How to fix?

Add a destroy() method to StructuredOutputBackend and implement it in xgrammar backend.
call this destroy() method when engine core shut down to dele

vllm.title: [V1][Spec Decoding] Add num_drafts and num_accepted_tokens_per_position metrics
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As discussed in the design doc.
Record num_drafts so that it can be used to calculate mean acceptance length.
Record num_accepted_tokens per draft position and log these as per-position acceptance probabilities. In Prometheus, use a Counter with a position label - somewhat similar to how a Histogram

vllm.title: Updating builkite job for IBM Power

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Modifying the buildkite job for IBM Power to use container-id instead of container name to run the test. This will help in running multiple jobs parallely without giving error related to container name "<name>" is already in use by <id>

vllm.title: existing torch installation pip command fix for docs
vllm.author: atilla00
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Move "--no-build-isolation" argument before dot for pip install. Saving time for people like me who copy paste commands from docs 😞.

vllm.title: Molmo Requirements
vllm.author: Eyshika
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add Molmo-specific requirements file for stable accuracy on different architectures.
Without these requirements user installs latest libs which doesnt provide same accuracy as it would on required libs.

vllm.title: Use custom address for listening socket
vllm.author: jglaser
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This sets up TCPStore to listen on a specified interface instead of on 0.0.0.0, which saves OS resources
Currently, using the old TCP store (use_libuv=False), as the new one (libuv=True) only supports custom socket setup in the most recent versions of Pytorch.
FIX #15987 (link existing issues this P

vllm.title: [FEAT] [ROCm]: AITER Fused MOE V1 Support
vllm.author: vllmellm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Description
This PR integrates enables Aiter's fused Mixture-of-Experts ops, found here,  to be used with v1.
Implementation
The following ops have been added/modified and registered as custom ops:

rocm_aiter_ck_moe
rocm_aiter_fmoe_fp8_blockscale_g1u1
rocm_aiter_asm_moe
rocm_aiter_topk_softmax
rocm

inference with float16:
from PIL import Image
from transformers import AutoProcessor
from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams

model_path = "moonshotai/Kimi-VL-A3B-Instruct"
llm = LLM(
    model_path,
    trust_remote_code=True,
    dtype="float16",
)

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Misc] Use TritonPlaceholderModule to defensively import triton
vllm.author: MengqingCao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #14888
FIX #16955
For devices not support triton, directly importing triton will break the inference. This pr offers a TritonPlaceholder and TritonLaguagePlaceholder module to hold the non-triton senario.
TritonPlaceholder and TritonLaguagePlaceholder will be injected to sys.modules to let syste

Support a flag for supporting appending to an existing results.json file, making benchmark sweeps like https://github.com/neuralmagic/benchmark-compare easier
Also fix save_detailed == False not filtering correctly

vllm.title: [Perf]Optimize rotary_emb implementation to use Triton operator for improved inference performance
vllm.author: cynthieye
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Optimize Rotary Positional Embeddings with Triton Kernel in VLLM
This PR enhances rotary positional embedding computation by leveraging Triton-optimized kernels from flash_attn, addressing a significant performance bottleneck observed in models like Qwen2-VL.
Background
The original PyTorch-native r
